page: Vultr vhp-2c-4gb-amd is a High Performance virtual server configured with 2 shared vCPUs mapped to 1 physical AMD EPYC core with 2 threads per core. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this instance runs at a CPU speed of 2.0 GHz and features 16 MB of L3 cache. The hardware configuration includes 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB per core, and is bundled with 100 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. One complimentary public IPv4 address is included with the deployment.

Benchmark data reveals a significant performance divergence between single-core and multi-core operations. The single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is strong, placing in the top 25% with a score of 2380.94. However, multi-core performance drops to the bottom 25% with a score of 4691.97, reflecting the shared nature of the CPU allocation. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with large-block read speeds of 20.62 GB/sec and write speeds of 21.58 GB/sec, both falling into the lower performance tiers. Database operations and Redis SET throughput are similarly limited, with Redis achieving 1.8 million operations per second.

Given its shared CPU architecture and modest memory capacity, this server offers a highly cost-efficient entry point for workloads that do not require sustained multi-core execution or high memory bandwidth. It is best suited for lightweight applications, basic web serving, development environments, and small-scale background tasks. It is not recommended for memory-intensive databases, heavy multi-threaded processing, or large-scale LLM inference.
description: The Vultr vhp-2c-4gb-amd is an entry-level virtual server from the High Performance family, featuring 2 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC processor clocked at 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of RAM and 100 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. While single-core CPU performance is strong, placing in the top 25% in stress-ng benchmarks, its shared CPU allocation and limited memory bandwidth of 20.62 GB/sec for large blocks result in weaker multi-core and database performance. This server represents a budget-friendly option for lightweight workloads, such as static web serving, software development testing, and basic background processing, where high resource density and sustained multi-threaded performance are not critical.
og_description: Vultr vhp-2c-4gb-amd is a High Performance virtual server featuring 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 100 GB of NVMe SSD storage, designed for budget-friendly, lightweight workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vhp-2c-4gb-amd is a shared AMD EPYC server with 2 vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and 100 GB NVMe storage, optimized for entry-level workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server combining strong single-core AMD EPYC performance with local NVMe storage for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs (1 physical core, 2 threads)
4.0 GB system memory and 100 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU performance placing in the top 25%
Shared CPU allocation with lower multi-core and memory bandwidth scaling
Best suited for static web serving, development environments, and lightweight tasks
